Politics.headline: Trump Cancels Envoys' Pakistan Trip for Iran Talks
Politics.detail: President Trump has called off his envoys' trip to Pakistan, where they were scheduled to participate in ceasefire negotiations with Iran, signaling a potential shift in diplomatic strategy. The reasons for the cancellation remain unclear.

Politics.headline: Security Incident at White House Correspondents' Dinner
Politics.detail: A security incident occurred at the White House Correspondents' Dinner, prompting a report from Xinhua News Agency. Details about the nature of the incident are still emerging.

Politics.headline: UK Assisted Dying Bill Fails After Delays
Politics.detail: A bill to legalize assisted dying for terminally ill individuals in the UK has failed after facing delays, though advocates have vowed to continue pushing for similar legislation in the future. The bill's supporters say it has a democratic mandate.

Politics.headline: Mounties, border officers and cyber spies shut out of early retirement incentive
Politics.detail: The Royal Canadian Mounted Police (Mounties), border officers, and cyber spies have been excluded from an early retirement incentive program. The details surrounding this decision are still emerging.

Politics.headline: NDIS reform: Minister Butler addresses scheme's future
Politics.detail: Minister Mark Butler discussed the government's plan to reform the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS), aiming to ensure its sustainability and improve value for money. The government aims to reduce the number of people on the scheme to around 600,000 by the end of the decade.

Finance & Economy.headline: Iran war already taking a toll on the global economy, IMF says in new forecast
Finance & Economy.detail: The International Monetary Fund cut its 2026 growth estimate but says an unusual amount of uncertainty clouds the outlook due to the ongoing conflict.

Finance & Economy.headline: UK eases airline penalties as jet fuel shortages threaten flights
Finance & Economy.detail: The UK government is relaxing penalties for airlines as jet fuel shortages are causing potential disruptions to flights.

Finance & Economy.headline: Alberta Energy Regulator orders MAGA Energy to suspend operations over 'failure to meet its commitments'
Finance & Economy.detail: The Alberta Energy Regulator has ordered oil and gas firm MAGA Energy Ltd. to suspend its operations over unresolved environmental concerns and non-compliance issues, including unpaid taxes.

Finance & Economy.headline: Trump Media replaces Nunes as Truth Social CEO after stock plunge that wiped out billions
Finance & Economy.detail: The Trump business behind Truth Social is replacing a former congressman and big supporter of the U.S. president as the leader of the social media platform after a stock collapse that wiped out billions in investor wealth.

Finance & Economy.headline: TikTok and Visa launch debit card to speed payouts to UK creators.
Finance & Economy.detail: TikTok and Visa have partnered to launch a debit card for UK creators, aiming to expedite payouts.

Technology.headline: Altman apologizes after OpenAI failed to alert police before Tumbler Ridge killings
Technology.detail: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has apologized after the company failed to alert police about a user who discussed planning a mass shooting on its platform prior to the event. The user later carried out the killings in Tumbler Ridge.

Technology.headline: Singapore emerging as neutral ground as AI firms navigate Sino-U.S. rivalry
Technology.detail: As tensions continue to rise between the United States and China, Singapore is positioning itself as a neutral hub for artificial intelligence development and collaboration, attracting AI firms from both countries.

Technology.headline: Crime crew impersonates help desk, abuses Microsoft Teams to steal your data
Technology.detail: A cybercrime group is using sophisticated social engineering techniques, impersonating help desk staff on Microsoft Teams to deploy custom malware and steal sensitive data from unsuspecting users.

Technology.headline: OpenAI CEO Apologizes for Not Warning Authorities About Mass Shooting Suspect
Technology.detail: Sam Altman, CEO of OpenAI, has issued an apology for the company's failure to alert law enforcement about a user who discussed plans for a mass shooting on the platform prior to the tragic event.

Technology.headline: More activity means less response in active materials
Technology.detail: A recent study reveals that increased activity in active materials can lead to a reduced response, impacting various technological applications.

Science.headline: Chornobyl: 40 Years After the Disaster
Science.detail: A personal account marks the 40th anniversary of the Chernobyl disaster, highlighting the impact on individual lives and the biodiversity that has thrived in the abandoned area. The story shares the experiences of a Kateryna Tushynska, whose life was largely framed by this disaster.

Science.headline: New Fossil from Brazil Reveals Unexpected Diversity among Pre-Dinosaur Herbivores
Science.detail: A newly discovered fossil from Brazil is shedding light on the unexpected diversity of herbivores that existed before the age of dinosaurs. The fossil provides new insights into the evolution of early herbivorous reptiles.

Science.headline: Marine Biologists Solve Mystery of Deep-Sea 'Golden Orb'
Science.detail: Marine biologists have successfully solved the mystery surrounding a deep-sea 'golden orb'. The discovery provides valuable information about the biodiversity and unique life forms found in the deep ocean.

Science.headline: HIV disrupts lung 'clock,' raising COPD and emphysema risk
Science.detail: Research indicates that HIV disrupts the lung's natural biological clock, which elevates the risk of developing chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) and emphysema. This finding highlights a new aspect of HIV's impact on lung health.

Science.headline: Scientists Just Discovered Africa Is Closer to Breaking Apart Than We Thought
Science.detail: Scientists have found that the Earth's crust beneath East Africa's Turkana Rift is thinning to a critical point, suggesting the continent is gradually breaking apart. This discovery provides insights into the geological processes shaping the African continent.

Health.headline: Harvard Scientists Link Gut Bacteria to Depression Through Hidden Inflammation Trigger
Health.detail: Researchers found that when Morganella morganii interacts with a common pollutant, it produces a molecule that may be quietly fueling depression.

Health.headline: Continued Insulin Pump Use in the Hospital Improves Outcomes
Health.detail: AACE 2026: New research suggests that continuing insulin pump use for patients in the hospital leads to improved outcomes.

Health.headline: Ancient Mass Grave Reveals How a Pandemic Wiped Out a City 1,500 Years Ago
Health.detail: A newly confirmed mass grave in ancient Jordan offers chilling insight into one of history's first pandemics, revealing how the Plague of Justinian devastated entire communities.

Health.headline: England Athletics promotion of medical knee procedure to runners on eve of London Marathon is condemned by experts
Health.detail: Experts are condemning England Athletics for promoting a medical knee procedure to runners just before the London Marathon.

Health.headline: Tobacco and Vapes Bill: UK ban on smoking by anyone born after 2008 is passed
Health.detail: The UK has passed a bill banning smoking for anyone born after 2008 in an effort to curb tobacco use.

Sports.headline: Kalyn Ponga re-signs with Newcastle Knights
Sports.detail: The skipper has extended his time at the club for a further three years, providing a boost for Newcastle ahead of their game against the Panthers.

Sports.headline: Sooryavanshi's Record Ton in Vain as SRH Chase Down 229 vs RR
Sports.detail: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's world record ton goes in vain as Sunrisers Hyderabad (SRH) chased down 229 against Rajasthan Royals (RR). Sooryavanshi also achieved the record for the fastest to 1,000 runs in T20s, smashing a 36-ball ton in the IPL match.

Sports.headline: Injured Salah to miss rest of Liverpool season, says Egypt national team director
Sports.detail: Mohamed Salah will miss the rest of Liverpool's season due to injury, according to the Egypt national team director.

Sports.headline: IPL 2026 DC vs PBKS | Marauding Kings willow-wielders scale newer heights
Sports.detail: Punjab Kings' batsmen displayed exceptional power hitting in their match against Delhi Capitals.

Sports.headline: Red Sox Fire Manager Alex Cora, Members of Coaching Staff Amid Poor Start
Sports.detail: The Boston Red Sox have fired manager Alex Cora and members of the coaching staff due to a poor start to the season.

Entertainment.headline: Deepika Padukone, Ranveer Singh announce second pregnancy
Entertainment.detail: Bollywood stars Deepika Padukone and Ranveer Singh have announced they are expecting their second child. The announcement was made via PTI news wire.

Entertainment.headline: I'm A Celebrity 'jungle legend' crowned in chaotic final
Entertainment.detail: Adam Thomas was crowned the winner of I'm A Celebrity, with the winner being announced by Sam Thompson, who won the original series in 2023. Thomas described being on the show as a “blast”.

Entertainment.headline: 'Bhooth Bangla' joins Rs 100 cr club; outshines 'Dhurandhar' and 'Michael'
Entertainment.detail: Akshay Kumar and Priyadarshan's 'Bhooth Bangla' has crossed Rs 100 crore in domestic collections and Rs 160 crore worldwide, outperforming competitors like 'Dhurandhar 2' and 'Michael'. The horror comedy had a strong opening and maintained momentum into its second week.

Entertainment.headline: Meg Accuses Now-Ex Klay of Cheating in Shocking Statement
Entertainment.detail: Meg has accused her now ex, Klay, of cheating in a shocking statement. The news has sparked discussions about their relationship and Klay's past dating history.

Entertainment.headline: Actor Jung Woo explores personal struggles and dreams in self-directed film 'Audition 109'
Entertainment.detail: Actor Jung Woo's self-directed film, 'Audition 109,' explores his personal struggles and dreams, with the protagonist inspired by his real-life nickname. The film, a sequel to the 2009 indie film 'Wish,' centers on an aspiring actor from Busan pursuing his dream in Seoul.

Crime & Justice.headline: Florida man charged with murder of university roommate, girlfriend
Crime & Justice.detail: Hisham Abugharbieh, a former University of South Florida student, has been charged with the premeditated murder of his roommate and his roommate's girlfriend, both doctoral students from Bangladesh who had disappeared earlier in April. Abugharbieh is being held without bond, and a hearing is set for April 28.

Crime & Justice.headline: A US bank robber's cellphone gave him up – but is geofencing legal?
Crime & Justice.detail: The Supreme Court is set to decide whether geofence warrants, which allow police to collect location history of cellphone users near a crime scene, violate the Fourth Amendment's ban on unreasonable searches. The case involves Okello Chatrie, who was apprehended using geofencing technology after robbing a bank in Virginia.

Crime & Justice.headline: YouTuber Arrested in 31-Year-Old Kidnapping and Murder Case
Crime & Justice.detail: Salim Wastik, a 54-year-old man known as a YouTuber, was arrested by the Delhi Police Crime Branch for a kidnapping and murder case dating back to 1995 after changing his identity. Wastik, a martial arts trainer at the victim's school, had been convicted in 1997 but evaded capture after being released on interim bail in 2000.

Crime & Justice.headline: Suspect arrested in murder of 15-year-old in Queens, NYPD says
Crime & Justice.detail: New York City police have charged Zahir Davis, 18, with the murder of 15-year-old Jaden Pierre at a park in St. Albans, Queens. The NYPD has seized two vehicles involved in a chaotic car meetup over the weekend in Maspeth, Queens.

Crime & Justice.headline: More than 370 charges laid by police over fuel theft, petrol drive-offs increase by 28%
Crime & Justice.detail: Police have laid more than 370 charges related to fuel theft, as petrol drive-offs have increased by 28%. There have been approximately 100 more petrol drive-offs reported to police each week.

Education.headline: NIT Silchar Regulates Religious Gatherings After Hostel Video Controversy
Education.detail: Following a controversy involving a video from a hostel, NIT Silchar has moved to regulate religious gatherings on campus to maintain order and prevent future incidents.

Education.headline: Goa Board GBSHSE Class 10 Result 2026 Date and Time: Board to declare results on April 26
Education.detail: The Goa Board of Secondary and Higher Secondary Education (GBSHSE) is set to declare the Class 10 results today, April 26, 2026.

Education.headline: Marks Or Skills? Delhi Business School Chairman Explains What Matters
Education.detail: The Chairman of Delhi Business School discusses the importance of skills versus marks in today's education and job market.

Education.headline: Los Angeles school board votes to set limits on classroom screen time
Education.detail: The Los Angeles school board has voted to implement limits on the amount of screen time allowed in classrooms, reflecting growing concerns about the impact of technology on learning.

Education.headline: Experts At NDTV Yuva On Upskilling, Mandatory Internship, Job Readiness
Education.detail: Experts at NDTV Yuva discussed the importance of upskilling, mandatory internships, and job readiness for Gen Z, emphasizing the need for practical skills and experience.

Lifestyle.headline: Your Weekly Horoscope, April 26-May 2, 2026: Making Sense of What's Next
Lifestyle.detail: This week's horoscope provides insights and guidance for navigating the upcoming days, offering advice on how to approach challenges and opportunities.

Lifestyle.headline: How to Befriend a Rabbit and Earn Its Trust (Vet Advice)
Lifestyle.detail: A veterinarian provides advice on how to properly befriend a rabbit and earn its trust through gentle interaction and understanding of their behavior.

Lifestyle.headline: 7 ways to get more exercise in your day as the cold weather hits
Lifestyle.detail: This article provides practical tips on incorporating more exercise into your daily routine during the cold weather season.

Lifestyle.headline: Helena Bonham Carter Exits 'The White Lotus' Season 4 - And Fans Aren't Happy
Lifestyle.detail: Helena Bonham Carter will not be appearing in the upcoming season of 'The White Lotus', disappointing many fans of the show.

Lifestyle.headline: Affordable Mother's Day Ideas at Home That Make the Day Feel Extra Meaningful Without Spending Much
Lifestyle.detail: The article suggests several affordable and meaningful ways to celebrate Mother's Day at home without breaking the bank.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL: KMT cheering for the wrong team
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The editorial discusses how China is obstructing Taiwan's foreign relations by pressuring countries to revoke overflight permissions for President William Lai's trip to Eswatini. It argues that Beijing's actions demonstrate insecurity and malice, rather than strength.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial: Second time surrender for Indonesia
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This editorial discusses how Indonesia has prioritized US strategic interests by signing the Agreement on Reciprocal Trade (ART) and allegedly approving blanket overflight access for US military aircraft. The ART places Indonesia in a subordinate position, requiring it to align with US trade policies.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Beijing's kinship is just abuse
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The article discusses the recent event where China pressured third countries to revoke overflight permissions for Taiwan's president, arguing it demonstrates a pattern of censoring and denying Taiwan's self-governing status. It criticizes Beijing for using economic leverage to interfere with the flight path of a democratically elected leader.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Close watch on how Trump and journalists will get along at White House correspondents' dinner
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Donald Trump's attendance at the White House Correspondents' Association dinner is putting his administration's contentious relationship with the press on display. Some news organizations are inviting sources as guests, including a former Trump official that the AP sued last year.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: African governments need to take urgent action on fertiliser shortages
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Tensions in the Strait of Hormuz have slowed fertiliser shipments which could put harvests at risk. This opinion piece urges African governments to take action.

World.headline: Trump Rushed Off Stage After Shots Fired at White House Correspondents Dinner
World.detail: President Donald Trump was rushed off stage during the White House Correspondents Dinner after shots were fired; the suspect is reportedly in custody and believed to be alive. There were no immediate reports of injuries.

World.headline: Latest ceasefire talks fail as Iran's top diplomat leaves Pakistan and Trump tells envoys not to go
World.detail: Ceasefire talks between the U.S. and Iran have stalled after Iran's foreign minister left Pakistan, leading President Trump to instruct U.S. envoys to cancel their trip. Tensions remain high as global trade is strained by disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz.

World.headline: Six killed in Israeli strikes on south Lebanon: Health Ministry
World.detail: Six people have been reported killed in Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon, according to the Health Ministry. This comes amid ongoing tensions and breaches of the ceasefire.

World.headline: Gunmen stage simultaneous attacks across Mali, army says
World.detail: Islamic militants and separatists have launched coordinated attacks in multiple locations across Mali, including the capital. The Malian army is responding to the attacks amidst rising security concerns.

World.headline: Explosion in southwest Colombia kills at least 14, state governor says
World.detail: An explosion on a bus in southwestern Colombia has resulted in the deaths of at least 14 people and injuries to 38. The incident underscores persistent violence in the region.

Zimbabwe.headline: Truck driver's licence cancelled for running over hitchhiker
Zimbabwe.detail: A truck driver had his license cancelled after running over a hitchhiker.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwean exotic leather producers tap into China's zero tariff policy
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwean producers of exotic leather are looking to take advantage of China's zero tariff policy.

Zimbabwe.headline: Pump revolt: ZiG currency turned away
Zimbabwe.detail: Reports indicate that some businesses are refusing to accept the ZiG currency.

Zimbabwe.headline: Man attempts suicide over 'headman post'
Zimbabwe.detail: A man reportedly attempted suicide due to issues surrounding a 'headman post'.

Zimbabwe.headline: Govt bows down to pressure over land tenure fees
Zimbabwe.detail: The government has apparently relented to pressure regarding land tenure fees.
